business.headline: Will Kwality Wall's big dairy push help it compete better in India?The company is making changes across products, pricing, factories and supply systems. Around half of Kwality Wall's products are expected to be dairy-based this year, while most of the shift will be completed next year.

Could Delhi HC's Hindware verdict reshape Google Ads and digital marketing in India?The Delhi High Court has ruled that Google cannot allow rival companies to bid on the trademarked keyword "HINDWARE" through its advertising platform.

Sensex, Nifty crash over inflation concern on poor rain forecastThe S&P BSE Sensex plunged 1,092.06 points, or 1.44%, to close at 74,775.74, while the NSE Nifty50 dropped 359.40 points, or 1.50%, to settle at 23,547.75.

Absolut Vodka maker battles Delhi ban as Rs 3,000 crore tax dispute deepensThe Delhi High Court has rejected Pernod Ricard's plea to resume liquor sales in the capital. The order adds to a separate customs dispute that has put nearly Rs 3,000 crore at stake.

How Gen Z and women buyers are turning India into the 2nd-largest diamond marketWhat if diamonds were no longer saved for "special occasions"? Across India, Gen Z shoppers and self-buying women are increasingly choosing diamonds as everyday luxury, a shift that is helping the country emerge as the world's second-largest diamond market.

Are India's stock investors overtaxed? Vijay Kedia thinks so, offers 3 solutionsVeteran investor Vijay Kedia has urged the Finance Ministry that the country's tax policy should reward long-term wealth creation instead of burdening those who provide patient capital to businesses.

From rising prices to rupee pressure: 5 key takeaways from RBI's annual reportIndia's economy remains strong, but challenges are growing. Slower growth, rising inflation risks and geopolitical tensions may test the economy in the coming months.

A war away, but not far away: RBI's warning for India's economyFor all the talk around India's strong economic growth, resilient consumers and healthy banks, the RBI's latest assessment contains a clear message: the biggest risks to the country's growth story are increasingly coming from outside its borders.

Under-construction or ready-to-move home: Which saves me more money in the long run?Buying a home is exciting, but one question often leaves buyers confused: should you go for a cheaper under-construction property or pay more for a ready-to-move home? Let's have a look.

Inspection Raj in the service of employers evading complianceIn the ninth part of this series on labour laws and Inspector Raj, this article examines how reduced scrutiny in the name of "ease of doing business" may create space for wilful labour law violations.

Why Delta Corp share price crashed 16% todayDelta Corp shares slid 16% after the Supreme Court upheld the government's retrospective 28 per cent GST levy on online gaming companies. The ruling has intensified concerns over large tax demands and compliance costs across the sector.

8th Pay Commission: Pensioners seek option to choose between OPS, NPS, UPSEmployee and pensioner groups have urged the 8th Pay Commission to let government staff choose between OPS, NPS and UPS. The demand has put retirement security and clarity over post-retirement income at the centre of the wider pay panel discussions.

Wipro jumps 5%, lifts IT stocks as AI deal sparks sector rallyWipro shares: Wipro stock gained as much as 4.76% during the day to Rs 211.20, while its American Depositary Receipts (ADRs) listed in the US had jumped more than 18% overnight.

IT stocks lead gains as Sensex, Nifty open higher on easing oil pricesThe S&P BSE Sensex rose 98.09 points, or 0.13%, to 75,965.89 in early trade, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 16.20 points, or 0.07%, to 23,923.35, as of 9:25 am.

SpaceX IPO: How Elon Musk's company is targeting a $1.8 trillion valuationSpaceX is discussing a valuation of about $1.8 trillion and a potential $75 billion raise ahead of its IPO. The filing shows the company is asking investors to see it as an AI infrastructure and space technology business.

Why India may be taking risky bet with $500 billion US dealMarco Rubio said in New Delhi that India had committed to buying $500 billion of American goods over five years. The remark has intensified scrutiny of whether the proposed arrangement offers India enough return.

The hidden salary leak Gen Z didn't notice until bank balances started shrinkingYoung salaried Indians are discovering that small app subscriptions are quietly eating into their monthly income. The online debate has sharpened focus on how auto-debits and convenience spending can distort budgeting.

Is E20 petrol quietly increasing your monthly fuel bill? 5 in 10 owners say yesA LocalCircles survey says many owners of older petrol vehicles have seen lower mileage after the E20 rollout. The findings sharpen consumer concerns over fuel costs, repairs and the shift towards higher ethanol blends.

Remote work is back in focus: Will it redefine how India chooses homes?Flexible work is again influencing how Indian homebuyers assess where to live. The shift could move demand beyond office hubs towards space, practicality and quality of life.

8th Pay Commission: Pension choice for government employees in coming monthsAt present, most central government employees recruited after January 1, 2004, fall under the National Pension System, which is contribution-based and linked to market performance.

entertainment.headline: Varun Dhawan seeks protection of personality rights from Delhi High Court Actor Varun Dhawan has secured interim protection from the Delhi High Court against the misuse of his identity online. The order comes amid rising concerns over deepfakes and unauthorised digital content involving celebrities.

House of the Dragon Season 3 final trailer promises bloodshed and betrayal The final trailer for House of the Dragon season 3 hints at devastating battles, shifting loyalties and major consequences for Westeros as the war for the Iron Throne intensifies.

Shahana Goswami admits to open relationship, multiple partners: Nothing is hidden Actor Shahana Goswami has opened up about being in an open relationship and why "nothing is hidden" from her partners. The actor also spoke about love, freedom and her past relationship with Milind Soman.

Film body calls for industry-wide guidelines amid Ranveer Singh–Don 3 'ban' row FWICE has approached major producer bodies over Ranveer Singh's reported exit from Don 3, proposing an industry-wide SOP to address last-minute actor withdrawals and financial disputes.

Hai Jawani Toh: Why David Dhawan, Vashu Bhagnani are fighting in Rs 400 crore case? Producer Vashu Bhagnani has moved court against filmmaker David Dhawan, Tips Industries Limited and the Tauranis over the alleged unauthorised use of songs from Biwi No. 1 in Hai Jawani Toh Ishq Hona Hai. He has also filed a Rs 400 crore claim against Tips.

Ankita Lokhande faces social media heat over photos in hijab from Abu Dhabi Ankita Lokhande's Abu Dhabi vacation post with husband Vicky Jain drew attention online after she shared photos wearing a hijab. The images sparked debate on social media, with several users reacting and making assumptions about her personal life.

Absolutely rubbish: Ananya Panday's dance mentor reacts to Bharatanatyam backlash Dancer and choreographer Sandip Soparrkar has reacted strongly to the criticism surrounding Ananya Panday's Bharatanatyam fusion performance in Chand Mera Dil, sparking a wider debate around classical dance and cinema.

Navjot Ahuja opens up about love, validation and the making of viral hit Khat Navjot Ahuja spoke about the inspiration behind Khat and his songwriting process. He said he measures success by creating honestly rather than seeking validation or chasing numbers.

Man arrested for planning Islamist attack at Taylor Swift's Vienna show A court in Austria has sentenced a man over the alleged Taylor Swift Vienna concert attack plot, reopening questions about the foiled terror scare that forced thousands of fans to miss the Eras Tour shows.

Deli Boys 2 review: Poorna Jagannathan steals the show again in messy madcapperRelease Date:Thu May 28, 2026 Mir and Raj Dar stumble through new power and dirty money in Deli Boys Season 2. Poorna Jagannathan's Auntie Lucky sharpens the chaos even as the narrative turns uneven.

The Pyramid Scheme: Paramvir Singh Cheema, Ranvir Shorey in a risky game of wealth The trailer of The Pyramid Scheme offers a gripping look into the dark world of pyramid marketing, greed, and the lure of quick money. Starring Paramvir Singh Cheema and Ranvir Shorey, the series follows two men whose ambitions slowly spiral into chaos.

Dhurandhar team drops production designer's credit amid sexual harassment case Production designer Saini S Johray was found guilty by an internal POSH committee months before his arrest in a Chandigarh sexual harassment case.

Rajni Ki Baraat review: Think twice before attending this baraatRelease Date:Fri May 29, 2026 Rajni Ki Baraat follows a Darbhanga girl fighting patriarchy for love. The film's bold climax and Ulka Gupta's restrained performance stand out, but you can't move beyond the uneven writing and forgettable music.

Actors Aditya Seal-Anushka Ranjan announce first pregnancy: Waited a hundred years Aditya Seal and Anushka Ranjan have announced that they are expecting their first child together after four years of marriage. The couple shared dreamy maternity pictures along with a heartfelt note about waiting "a hundred years" for the special moment.

Rs 10,000 a month for house-helps? Mini Mathur joins Kirti Kulhari in viral debate Mini Mathur criticised Kirti Kulhari's resurfaced remarks about paying a domestic worker Rs 10,000 for two hours of daily work. The clip has reignited debate over domestic help wages, living costs and expectations in Mumbai.

Vashu Bhagnani sues David Dhawan for Rs 400 crore in Hai Jawani Toh... songs row Vashu Bhagnani has sued David Dhawan, Tips Industries and Ramesh Taurani for Rs 400 crore over the use of songs from Biwi No. 1 in the upcoming film Hai Jawani Toh Ishq Hona Hai.

22 years later, Hum Tum's Kunal Kohli calls Saif-Rani's iconic kiss cinema's worst Filmmaker Kunal Kohli revisited Hum Tum after 22 years and recalled the awkward kissing scene between Saif Ali Khan and Rani Mukerji. The filmmaker joked that the iconic moment turned out to be "one of the worst kisses of Hindi cinema."

This 33-year-old Indian actor just beat Vijay, SRK on IMDb's most popular list Actor Yudhvir Ahlawat has drawn wide attention for playing Harpal in Netflix's Kartavya alongside Saif Ali Khan. Viewers mistook the 33-year-old for a child artiste, sending his IMDb popularity sharply upwards.

CM Vijay swaps black blazer for silk veshti-shirt at manager's housewarming Tamil Nadu Chief Minister Vijay attended his manager Jagadish Palanisamy's housewarming in Chennai wearing a traditional silk veshti and shirt. The appearance drew attention as it was different from his usual black blazer style.

general news.headline: Trump claims Hormuz blockade over; Iran adds conditions to passage reopeningTrump signalled that the US naval blockade linked to the Strait of Hormuz could be lifted under a potential deal with Iran, while Tehran maintained that any reopening of the strategic waterway would be governed by its own conditions and operational control.

GT vs RR Qualifier 2 LIVE: Gill, Sai give Gujarat solid start in 215 chaseGujarat Titans (GT) vs Rajasthan Royals (RR) Live Score, IPL 2026 Qualifier 2: GT (59/0 in 5 ov), chasing 215. Shubman Gill and Sai Sudharsan have begun Gujarat's 215-run chase. Earlier, another special effort from the remarkable 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, followed by a late onslaught from Donovan Ferreira, powered Rajasthan Royals to a formidable 214-run total and put them within touching distance of the IPL 2026 final. Royals looked in trouble after early strikes from Mohammed Siraj and Kagiso Rabada, but Ravindra Jadeja steadied the innings with a counter-attacking knock and a crucial partnership with Sooryavanshi before an elbow injury forced him to retire hurt.

Siddaramaiah pushes for coordination panel role in bid to checkmate DKSSiddaramaiah has proposed a coordination committee to link the Congress and the Karnataka government after stepping down as Chief Minister.

Did CBSE ask principals to defend OSM system? Videos fuel toolkit chargeAmid growing complaints over CBSE's On-Screen Marking (OSM) system, schools were allegedly sent a toolkit with suggested scripts and talking points to defend the process, raising fresh questions about transparency.

Not Shiva-Pashupati on Harappan seal? US historian Audrey Truschke sparks outrageAmerican historian Audrey Truschke challenged the Ministry of Culture's description of the Harappan Pashupati seal as "Shiva-Pashupati". She triggered a row by saying that the figure on the seal was "not Shiva", but the image was "more likely adapted from proto-Elamite iconography". Truschke's remark triggered a wider debate on archaeology, civilisational continuity and who gets to interpret India's ancient past.

Two days, 2 centuries missed: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi livid with himself after 96 vs GTVaibhav Sooryavanshi missed out on another IPL hundred by just four runs as he delivered another batting masterclass during Qualifier 2 against Gujarat Titans. The RR opener was livid with himself as he made his way off the field after having missed out on a ton against SRH in the Eliminator.

When Israel, India planned to strike Pak's Islamic bomb site; Why Indira said noAs Pakistan moved dangerously close to an "Islamic bomb" in the early 1980s, India and Israel explored a covert strike on Kahuta to prevent it from having a nuclear weapon. But why did PM Indira Gandhi call off the mission?

Fleeing Bangladeshis reveal how they illegally crossed into IndiaIllegal Bangladeshi migrants, who are flocking to the Bangladesh border amid a crackdown in West Bengal, are describing how middlemen moved them into India and helped them in securing documents. Here's the entire trail, reconstructed from the admissions by the illegal immigrants themselves.

20 years on death row, Kerala man returns from Saudi Arabia as blood money is paidKerala native Abdul Rahim returned to his hometown on Eid after spending nearly 20 years on death row in a Saudi Arabian prison. He was pardoned following the payment of Rs 34 crore in blood money, which was raised through a crowdfunding campaign by Malayalis.

Inside Story: Why Rishabh Pant walked away from LSG captaincyInside story of how growing differences with the team management, a difficult season and concerns over his form culminated in Rishabh Pant stepping down as Lucknow Super Giants captain.

Why was toss retaken during RR vs GT IPL Qualifier 2? ExplainedA missed call from Riyan Parag forced the toss to be taken again before Rajasthan Royals chose to bat. The muddled start left Shubman Gill irritated and put officiating under an awkward early spotlight.

BJP vs Congress over CBSE vendor row: X spat turns into political battleAmit Malviya defended CBSE's On Screen Marking system as Rahul Gandhi questioned the tender process, triggering a war on X that has pushed the row beyond implementation concerns to a larger debate over transparency and accountability.

Arash-e Kamangir: Iran's new weapon that it claims destroyed US $30 million droneIran said it used its new Arash-e Kamangir air defence system to bring down a US MQ-9 Reaper drone near the Strait of Hormuz, a claim that has reignited debate over how much of Tehran's air defence network remains operational after repeated Israeli and US strikes.

Claude AI bills getting out of control, company spends $500 mn in just one monthCompanies are embracing AI for work. But AI tools like Claude come at a cost, which can sometimes even shock you. As per reports, one company ended up spending $500,000,000 in a single month on Claude AI after it failed to set usage limits.

El Nino to hit India during monsoon, last until September, says IMD chiefFor India, the developments are particularly significant because El Nino has historically been associated with weaker monsoon rainfall, frequent heatwaves and increased stress on agriculture and water resources.

Rain, thunderstorms, hail likely across India on Saturday, heat to go downThe IMD has forecast thunderstorms, rain, gusty winds and hail across large parts of India on Saturday. The spell is expected to ease recent heatwave conditions, though localised severe weather may disrupt travel.

No hair, no attendance? Telangana worker finds unusual face-scan fixA Telangana MGNREGA worker with a freshly shaved head was not recognised by the attendance app until he came up with a unique fix. The incident has raised fresh questions about how appearance changes can affect facial recognition systems.

TMC exodus deepens as Assam chief quits, claims party works only for MuslimsAbhijit Majumdar resigned as Trinamool Congress chief in Assam and accused the party of working only for Muslims in Assam and West Bengal. His exit adds to a series of resignations and exposes deeper unrest after Bengal election setback.

DK Shivakumar's likely Cabinet takes shape; Siddaramaiah loyalists among ministersA list of 15 likely names has surfaced for Karnataka's new cabinet under DK Shivakuamar, reflecting power balancing, caste equations and continuity.

Siddaramaiah's quiet revenge on DKS and Rahul GandhiJust before stepping down as the Chief Minister of Karnataka, Siddaramaiah decided to accept a caste census report, pending since November 2025. With this, he is leaving a ticking bomb for his successor, DK Shivakumar, and the Congress's de facto chief, Rahul Gandhi.

NEET, CBSE and Rs 1,000 crore mess: Expensive lesson that India didn't ask forThe NEET fallout and CBSE's Class 12 evaluation controversy have highlighted the escalating financial cost of exam failures. The episode has renewed questions over why authorities spend heavily on damage control instead of planning for prevention.

Indian-origin Shrey Parikh spelt this word to win US Spelling BeeShrey Parikh beat fellow Indian-origin finalist Ishaan Gupta in a tense lightning-round tiebreaker to win the Scripps National Spelling Bee, the United States' largest and longest-running educational competition.

Video of SP leader's brutal attack on district women's wing chief sparks outrageSamajwadi Party women's wing leader Gargi Patel was allegedly assaulted at her Chandauli home during a dispute linked to a property business meeting. Police have registered an FIR against five people and are examining CCTV footage to establish the sequence of events and motive.

Nisarga Adhikary hacks CBSE portal again, plays viral Bad Apple songNisarga Adhikary, a 19-year-old ethical hacker, has sparked fresh controversy around CBSE's digital systems after claiming he managed to play the viral Bad Apple animation meme on a CBSE portal, while demonstrating what he described as serious security vulnerabilities.

sports.headline: Sooryavanshi breaks more records in GT vs RR despite 96 heartbreak IPL 2026: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi struck a record-laden knock for Rajasthan Royals against Gujarat Titans in Qualifier 2. Even without a hundred, the innings underlined the scale and range of his IPL 2026 season.

WFI draws line: Vinesh Phogat cleared only for 50kg trials The Wrestling Federation of India (WFI) has cleared Vinesh Phogat to compete in the Asian Games selection trials, but only in the 50kg category, adding a fresh layer to the ongoing controversy.

Two days, 2 centuries missed: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi livid with himself after 96 vs GT Vaibhav Sooryavanshi missed out on another IPL hundred by just four runs as he delivered another batting masterclass during Qualifier 2 against Gujarat Titans. The RR opener was livid with himself as he made his way off the field after having missed out on a ton against SRH in the Eliminator.

Aditi Chauhan, MM Somaiya to be government observers for Vinesh Phogat's Asiad trial Backed by the Supreme Court, Vinesh Phogat will compete in the Asian Games selection trials on Saturday with IOA and Sports Ministry observers present, ensuring transparency in a closely watched wrestling showdown.

Vaibhav Sooryavanshi scripts massive T20 powerplay record in IPL 2026 IPL 2026: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi added another milestone to his remarkable season by becoming the first batter in T20 history to score 500 runs in the powerplay of a single tournament. The 15-year-old achieved the feat during RR's Qualifier 2 clash against Gujarat Titans.

Why was toss retaken during RR vs GT IPL Qualifier 2? Explained A missed call from Riyan Parag forced the toss to be taken again before Rajasthan Royals chose to bat. The muddled start left Shubman Gill irritated and put officiating under an awkward early spotlight.

ISSF World Cup: Aishwary Pratap Tomar disqualified after equipment check failure Aishwary Pratap Singh Tomar was disqualified from the ISSF World Cup men's 50m rifle 3 positions final after a post-competition equipment check revealed a measurement discrepancy in his rifle. The decision stood despite an Indian appeal and could potentially dent his chances of securing selection for the Asian Games.

Inside Story: Why Rishabh Pant walked away from LSG captaincy Inside story of how growing differences with the team management, a difficult season and concerns over his form culminated in Rishabh Pant stepping down as Lucknow Super Giants captain.

Lakshya Sen follows PV Sindhu out of Singapore Open, falls in quarters Lakshya Sen lost a draining Singapore Open quarterfinal to Koki Watanabe after fading in the decider. His exit deepened India's singles disappointment, but the doubles campaign kept India's challenge alive.

Roland Garros under fire after Zeynep Sonmez suffers freak injury on court Zeynep Sonmez retired from her French Open doubles match after colliding with a Lacoste structure behind the baseline. The injury has sharpened criticism of Roland Garros for keeping branding obstacles so close to the court.

Pooja Singh breaks high-jump national record to clinch Asian U20 gold Pooja Singh won gold in the Asian U20 Athletics Championships with a 1.93m high jump. The effort broke India's long-standing national record and pushed her past the Commonwealth Games qualifying mark.

Paraguayan player faces heat after controversial remark on female chair umpireFrench Open 2026: Adolfo Vallejo has come under heavy criticism after controversial remarks about female chair umpire Ana Carvalho following his five-set loss to teenage sensation Moise Kouame.

Rishabh Pant steps down as LSG captain after disastrous IPL 2026IPL 2026: After a disastrous IPL 2026 campaign, Rishabh Pant has decided to step down as the Lucknow Super Giants skipper with immediate effect. Lucknow was the first team eliminated from IPL 2026 and won just four matches all season.

GT vs RR Qualifier 2 LIVE: Gill, Sai give Gujarat solid start in 215 chaseGujarat Titans (GT) vs Rajasthan Royals (RR) Live Score, IPL 2026 Qualifier 2: GT (52/0 in 4 ov), chasing 215. Shubman Gill and Sai Sudharsan have begun Gujarat's 215-run chase. Earlier, another special effort from the remarkable 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, followed by a late onslaught from Donovan Ferreira, powered Rajasthan Royals to a formidable 214-run total and put them within touching distance of the IPL 2026 final. Royals looked in trouble after early strikes from Mohammed Siraj and Kagiso Rabada, but Ravindra Jadeja steadied the innings with a counter-attacking knock and a crucial partnership with Sooryavanshi before an elbow injury forced him to retire hurt.

Serena Williams plans Queen's comeback, Canadian teen excited for dream partnershipSerena Williams is reportedly considering a sensational comeback at Queen's Club alongside rising Canadian teenager Victoria Mboko, reigniting speculation about the 23-time Grand Slam champion's return to tennis.

Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's exploits has ex-Australian cricketer thinking of BradmanQualifier 2, GT vs RR: Tom Moody invoked Don Bradman while describing Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's remarkable rise, saying the 15-year-old is producing something cricket fans have never witnessed before.

Satwik-Chirag into Singapore Open semis, Sindhu ousted by two-time Olympic championSingapore Open 2026: Satwik-Chirag kept India's Singapore Open hopes alive by reaching the semi-finals, while PV Sindhu bowed out after a straight-games defeat to world No. 1 An Se Young in the quarter-finals.

How Yastika Bhatia fought back from ACL Injury to earn Women's T20 World Cup spotWomen's T20 World Cup: Yastika Bhatia returned from a serious ACL injury with a fluent 54 against England in the first women's T20I. The innings, backed by Jemimah Rodrigues and debutant Nandini Sharma, underlined India's growing depth before the World Cup.

Mentally stressed Hardik Pandya is done with Mumbai IndiansHardik Pandya is reportedly set to leave the Mumbai Indians after a mentally exhausting and disappointing IPL 2026 season, having informed the franchise of his decision midway through the campaign.

Frances Tiafoe makes promise to fan who sneakily ran away with his racketFrench Open 2026: Frances Tiafoe became the talk of Roland Garros after a fan ran away with his racket during post-match celebrations following his marathon win over Hubert Hurkacz.

Day 6 Live Updates: Djokovic takes 2nd set vs Fonseca, Swiatek into prequartersFrench Open 2026 Live Updates: Novak Djokovic has taken the first set against Joao Fonseca as the Brazilian showed some fight against the third seed. Earlier on in the day, Swiatek reached the pre-quarters stage of the competition.

Reminds of Shahid Afridi: Ex-Pak player faces backlash over Sooryavanshi claimIPL 2026: Mohammad Yousaf compared Vaibhav Sooryavanshi to a young Shahid Afridi after the teenager's brutal IPL knock. The praise drew a sharp backlash, showing how quickly hype around a prodigy turns into argument.

SC clears Vinesh Phogat for Asian Games trials, but with a stern messageVinesh Phogat can compete in the Asian Games trials after the Supreme Court refused to stay a Delhi High Court order, though it sharply questioned the reasoning behind the ruling.

Thriller Night in Paris: Sabalenka responds to Djokovic with MJ-inspired dance movesFrench Open 2026: Novak Djokovic and Aryna Sabalenka have turned their French Open progress into a playful dance exchange in Paris. It has added levity to a tournament that has opened up further after Jannik Sinner's early exit.

Juan Cerundolo dumps Jannik Sinner out of French Open, elder brother left stunnedFrench Open 2026: Francisco Cerundolo was left stunned in Paris as he learned mid-match that his younger brother Juan had produced a sensational five-set comeback to knock out World No.1 Jannik Sinner at Roland Garros.

Don't look at his age: Gavaskar believes that Sooryavanshi is ready for India call-upSunil Gavaskar believes Vaibhav Suryavanshi is ready for India's T20 side, urging selectors to ignore his age and back the fearless batting prodigy after his stunning rise in cricket.

Ballon d'Or leaves Paris for London as award celebrates 70th anniversaryThe Ballon d'Or ceremony will leave Paris for London on October 26 in its 70th anniversary year. The move gives the awards a symbolic English marker, with Harry Kane, Ousmane Dembele and Aitana Bonmati shaping the contest.

Singapore Open Highlights: Lakshya Sen follows PV Sindhu exits in quarters, Sat-Chi and Dhruv-Kapila advanceSingapore Open 2026 Highlights: Lakshya Sen crashes out after losing 19-21, 21-15, 15-21 to Japanese Koki Watanabe in the quarterfinals. Earlier, Satwiksairaj Rankireddy and Chirag Shetty defeated the Malaysian pair, Aaron Tai and Khai Xing Kang in the men's doubles quarterfinals clash.

Day 6 Schedule: Novak Djokovic, Iga Swiatek aim prequarters berthFrench Open 2026: Day 6 at Roland Garros 2026 features a stacked third-round schedule, with Novak Djokovic and Iga Swiatek headlining the action as they aim to reach the pre-quarterfinals in Paris. N Sriram Bajali and Yuki Bhambri will also be in action.

Ban on smart glasses in IPL? BCCI announces tighter security protocolsIPL 2026: BCCI's Anti-Corruption Unit has banned smart glasses in IPL 2026 dressing rooms and restricted areas, citing security and integrity concerns. Players and staff must deposit the devices on match days.

Keep winning, then no one can deny me: Alex Perez eyes UFC title redemptionAlex Perez heads into the Su Mudaerji fight in Macau insisting his only job is to keep winning. After injury, defeats and a contract cliff-edge, he believes another run will make the UFC take notice.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
